claimText: Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) can be blended up to 50% with conventional aviation turbine fuel.
evidenceExcerpt: Can be blended up to 50% in conventional aviation turbine fuel (ATF or jet fuel).
examUse: Statement-based question on the technical properties and blending limits of SAF.
distractorHooks: SAF can only be blended up to 10% or 20% similar to ethanol
SAF requires completely modified engines and cannot be blended with conventional ATF

claimText: India has mandated a 1% Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) blending in jet fuel for international airlines starting in 2027.
evidenceExcerpt: India mandated 1% SAF blending in jet fuel sold to international airlines from 2027.
examUse: Statement-based question on India's renewable energy and aviation emission targets.
distractorHooks: The mandate applies to domestic airlines

claimText: Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) is produced from non-petroleum feedstocks, including algae, agricultural residues, and wastewater treatment sludge.
evidenceExcerpt: Raw material: Corn grain, Oil seeds, Algae, Agricultural residues, Forestry residues, Wastewater treatment sludge etc.
examUse: Multiple-choice question identifying valid feedstocks for sustainable aviation fuel production.
distractorHooks: SAF is a highly refined petroleum product
Wastewater treatment sludge cannot be used for aviation fuel
SAF relies exclusively on edible crops
